title: 10 possible side effects of Salvia miltiorrhiza (Dan Shen) if used improperly
intro: Salvia miltiorrhiza, also known as Danshen, is a plant commonly used in traditional Chinese medicine. If used improperly, it can potentially cause a number of health issues. First, overdosing on this herb can lead to stomach irritation, nausea, and vomiting. Additionally, it may cause low blood sugar and blood pressure, which can be dangerous for certain individuals. In rare cases, it has been linked to liver damage and abnormal heart rhythms. Pregnant women should avoid it as it may stimulate uterine contractions and lead to miscarriage. Lastly, it can interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners, increasing the risk of bleeding.
